intent: Trace the GET /notes/search flow that builds a raw SQL query from the user-supplied `q` argument. The annotation set must reach the f-string interpolation site.
Trace the POST /webhooks/test flow showing how the user-supplied URL reaches the outbound httpx call.
Trace GET /users/{user_id}. The handler resolves any user by integer id with no comparison against the caller; identify the entrypoint and the service-layer lookup the integer id reaches.
Trace GET /users/{user_id}/notes — the same integer id selects which user's notes are returned. Annotate the handler and the service-layer listing call.
Trace DELETE /notes/{note_id}. The handler looks up the note by id and deletes it; the path from user input to the delete sink runs through NoteService.get and NoteService.delete.
Trace POST /files/upload. The handler constructs the storage path from `file.filename` (client-supplied). Annotate the entrypoint and any helper that touches the filename before it reaches `open()`.
Trace GET /admin/users. The handler enumerates every user; identify the entrypoint and the service-layer enumeration call. Verify whether any auth/role check gates the handler.

Trace POST /auth/register. Follow the request body through the service-layer registration and into the response object. The response shape returns the bcrypt password_hash to the caller; the annotation must reach the service call and ideally the model that carries the hash.

Trace POST /auth/password/reset/confirm. The handler delegates to AuthService.confirm_password_reset which checks the OTP code by equality. Annotate the entrypoint and the verification function and note whether attempt counting / rate limit guards the check.

Trace POST /notes. The handler accepts a cookie-authenticated state-changing request. Annotate the entrypoint and the service-layer create call; note whether any anti-CSRF token / Origin / Referer check is present.
